title: Oxford Study: Rapamycin Rejuvenates Immune System, Reduces T Cell DNA Damage
description: University of Oxford researchers find mTOR inhibition via rapamycin doubles T cell survival and reduces DNA damage in older adults, published in Aging Cell.

title: Daily Multivitamin May Slow Biological Aging by Four Months
description: COSMOS trial data shows daily multivitamin slowed all five epigenetic clocks, equating to about four months less biological aging over two years.

title: Vitamin C Identified as Direct Inhibitor of ACSL4, Alleviating Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Landmark primate study defines ferro-aging as a core aging mechanism and shows long-term vitamin C reverses biological age through ACSL4 inhibition.

title: ARPA-H Awards $144M for PROSPR Program to Extend Healthspan
description: Seven research teams receive up to $144M from ARPA-H to build phase 3 trial infrastructure for aging interventions including rapamycin and semaglutide.

title: Columbia ARPA-H PROSPR Contract: Rapamycin May Slow Ovarian Aging by 20%
source: Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health
description: Columbia receives PROSPR award to analyze existing trial biospecimens; preliminary results suggest rapamycin may slow ovarian aging by approximately 20%.

title: Longevity Health Holdings Appoints New CEO, Closes Strategic Investment
description: Longevity Health Holdings (OTCQB: XAGE) appoints Ram Ajjarapu as Chairman and CEO to drive acquisition-focused growth strategy.

title: Biohackers World 2026 LA Conference Set for March 28-29
description: Over 35 speakers and 75 exhibitors will cover longevity science, metabolic health, biomarkers, and AI in health data at InterContinental LA Downtown.

title: ABC News Covers Multivitamin Aging Research on Good Morning America
description: Dr. Darien Sutton breaks down new research suggesting daily multivitamins may slow biological aging, bringing COSMOS trial findings to mainstream audience.

title: Longevity Science: From Biohacking Hype to Measurable Biology
description: In-depth analysis of how longevity science is maturing into systems biology of aging, covering epigenetic clocks, organ-specific aging trajectories, and NAD metabolism.

title: Biotech Lands $45M to Drug the Undruggable
description: New funding round supports development of UNP's platform to advance multiple lead programs toward clinical development in previously undruggable targets.

title: Polyamines: Anti-Aging Compound May Also Fuel Cancer via eIF5A2
description: Researchers uncover how polyamines boost healthy cells via eIF5A1 but turbocharge cancer through nearly identical protein eIF5A2, revealing context-dependent dual roles.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Comprehensive roundup featuring Altos Labs ($3B), Retro Biosciences, NewLimit, Cambrian Bio, and other leading longevity startups with latest funding details.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising: $6.9B Concentrated in Top 5
description: Analysis of longevity startup funding reveals Altos Labs, Oura, Retro Biosciences, BioSplice, and Insilico Medicine capture roughly $6.9B of the sector's capital.

title: Caring for the Body Launches Re-Think Aging Platform for Cognitive Decline Prevention
description: Physician-led platform launches educational podcast and clinical pathways aimed at cognitive decline prevention and longevity care.

title: Steve Horvath Highlights March 2026 as Strong Month for Longevity Supplement Research
description: Epigenetic clock pioneer notes COSMOS multivitamin trial and ferro-aging vitamin C study make March 2026 a standout month for longevity supplement research.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytic and NK Cell Advances
description: Monthly roundup highlights Nature Aging paper on mitochondrial stress boosting senolytic effectiveness, and Aging Cell study on restoring natural killer cell function.

title: Rapamycin for Longevity: Pros, Cons, and Future Perspectives
description: Comprehensive review summarizing mTOR pathway, PEARL trial results, and gaps in knowledge on chronic mTOR inhibition for healthy human aging.

title: Senolytics for Aging 2026: Dasatinib, Quercetin, and Fisetin Protocols
description: Practical guide to senolytic therapy in 2026, covering clinical evidence for D+Q and fisetin, Mayo Clinic trial results, and current dosing protocols.

title: WHOOP Enters $34.5M ARPA-H-Backed Bid to Quantify Aging
description: Wearable company WHOOP joins ARPA-H PROSPR program with $34.5M backing to develop aging quantification tools using continuous health monitoring data.

title: Richter Sharpens Ovarian Aging Focus Following Celmatix Asset Deal
description: Richter acquires Celmatix assets to advance ovarian aging research, joining growing pharma interest in reproductive longevity space.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Hit $45.8B by 2033
description: Market research projects longevity biotech growing at 20.4% CAGR, with key players including Calico Labs, Altos Labs, Unity Biotechnology, and Life Biosciences.

title: Wearable Startup Temple Secures $54M for Brain Monitoring
description: Brain monitoring wearable startup Temple raises $54M to advance neurotechnology relevant to cognitive aging and neurodegenerative disease detection.

title: Mimio Health Trial Shows Fasting-Mimetic Delivers Benefits Without Dieting
description: Clinical trial data shows Mimio Health's fasting-mimetic supplement delivers metabolic benefits of fasting without requiring actual dietary restriction.

title: Global Longevity Federation 2026 Conference: Rome, March 23-24
description: Global experts will convene in Rome to discuss longevity, healthy aging, and regenerative medicine at the annual GLF hybrid event.

title: Eric Verdin on CD38 Inhibitors and the Future of Aging Research
description: Buck Institute President discusses CD38 inhibitors that increase lifespan in mice, creatine for mitochondrial health, and NAD metabolism advances.
